metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_125
metadata.comment:
Rationale: As a motor adapter, FEZ1 functions to link syntaxin-1 to the Kinesin-1 motor. Loss of FEZ1 (UNC-76) in C. elegans led to the abnormal of syntaxin-1 along neurons axons which is different from its usual distribution along axonal plasma membranes. Expression of the FEZ1/UNC-76 phospho-mutant also resulted in the same phenotype.

In a subsequent paper (Butkevich et al., 2016; PMID 27247180), loss of FEZ1 function either in the unc-76 mutant or loss of the activating kinase PAR-1/MARK was further shown to lead to loss of presynaptic specialisations.

These data highlight a role of FEZ1 in establishing presynaptic specialisations via transporting presynaptic components.. Experimental description: Legend to Fig. 4. "Mutation of FEZ1 (unc-76) affects trafficking of syntaxin (UNC-64) in ventral nerve cords of C. elegans."

3/2/2017 Pim:
-Uniprot lists 10(!) unreviewed sequences for C.elegans FAZ1. I selected the accession with the MW as close to the human FEZ1 protein as posible.
